title: salvia haematodes subsp. tiberina
intro: Salvia haematodes subsp. Tiberina, also known as the Tiberian red sage or blood-sage, is a subspecies of the Salvia haematodes plant, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is a medicinal herb that has been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. The health benefits of Salvia haematodes subsp. Tiberina include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and act as an expectorant to relieve congestion. The herb can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region where it is native.
